content: The open-ended, exploratory memory expansion mechanism of 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agents can be reframed as a recursive self-generalization process where the agent itself acts as a dynamic, rewritable memory structure rather than a separate entity that stores memories in a static external bank. This means agent performance improves not just by adding new memories to a fixed architecture, but by continuously rewriting the agent’s core decision boundaries via active selection of which memories to integrate.
